Good platform, terrible app I’ve been a Notion power user since about 2018. I rely on it for hours every day. The overall trajectory of the platform has been great since then, but the mobile app has always had issues. And it seems they are getting worse. Just about every app update these days introduces major workflow breaking bugs. For example, it worked perfectly yesterday until I updated to the latest version today, and now mobile dictation is completely broken. 

Currently, with many multi select properties, sometimes opening the selection window completely crashes the app and it must be manually restarted.

Each update is like Russian Roulette: perhaps you’ll gain some small feature, and hopefully you won’t also get a new bug that completely interferes with usability. A bit hyperbolic? Sure. My business runs on this platform and bugs that break basic usability cost me. 

Will update this review if they manage to roll out 3 consecutive updates without breaking anything major.

When I had clearly selected a line, it would suddenly start typing on a completely different line. Also whenever I tried deleting an emoji in a preset, it would not let me. That brings me to another point. I personally think that they are trying to make this appealing for a younger audience by having an absolute dump of emojis. Another significant downside is that it is not possible to scroll with an Apple Pencil. As without the Apple Pencil, the UI was really buggy and would randomly jump from window to window. Also, the starter notions couldn’t be deleted. Overall, its a very disappointing app after seeing all of the positive marketing and reviews. I would not suggest this app. Instead of this you should just use the built in notes app. I have tried a lot of note apps in the past but I came to the realization that the built in notes app is the best because its perfectly adapted with iOS and all apple devices. So if you are looking for a good note app that does everything, you should just opt for the built in one. I am writing this review for the information of others and I hope this will help you make the right choice. Although this is my personal view and yours may be different. So even after reading this, and all the other bad reviews, I still encourage you to try this app and see how it works for your personal needs.

Amazing app, although the features are a bit clunky and unintuitive at times Eg. the menu for blocks is a little confusing to navigate, and the meaning and functions behind the property icons are a bit unclear.
Other than that, this app is absolutely jam-packed with features, which I love. However, this can also cause the experience to be a little finicky, and things are hard to find at times. 
The offline feature is very useful, as well as the capability to seamlessly sync across an unlimited amount of devices. I’d like to give a huge thank you for these; it’s so frustrating when other apps require you to subscribe to their paid plan in order to access these features.

Nobody can take such risks with their data Notion is an amazing concept. The combination of markdown, databases, different views, and linking is extremely powerful and inspiring, and the friendly interface makes you feel right at home.

Unfortunately, nobody should be actually using this app in reality. All your data lives online ONLY (except for whatever happens to be cached at the moment). That is, Notion doesn’t just sync your stuff between devices, like other apps, but they keep it on their own server and you merely get to access it on demand. If the internet’s off, your stuff is inaccessible and app features such as templates stop working. This year, they’ve had two major outages already — and I’m writing this in February. 

People have been demanding an offline mode for well over a year and while it was initially promised by developers, they have simply stopped mentioning it.

Furthermore, the app suffers speed and stability issues, and you should think twice about using it for “everything” as it is intended. Not just because you may lose access to it at any time and without warning, but also because it becomes very hard to switch. So if they go bust, or if you get fed up with playing roulette with the access to your data, you’ll be stuck with your entire digital life in a system that you can’t easily move elsewhere.

Finally, check out the privacy labels in the App Store, and *think*.

I love the proof of concept, though.

The other concern is that I’m hearing a lot about Notion’s lack of security and privacy. That’s not good. :(

Not enough customization; poor compatibility with Apple Accessibility features At a first glance, Notion has everything in terms of customization - multiple colours, background colours, ability to embed videos and choose avatars for each of the projects. Unfortunately, a more important customization features are completely ignored - and that is the ability to choose font size. I want my to-do lists to be larger than a regular text, allowing me to check items on the go. There is just to option for that. And that’s disappointing. 

Moreover, changing the font size for the app in Accessibility settings of iPhone doesn’t translate well in the app itself - affecting only the text in some of the menus and headings of the pages. Not sure why, but I’m guessing something is off with the implementation on Notion’s side. In theory, all apps should react to these settings properly - at least that’s what Apple promised when they introduced the feature with iOS16.

I will be willing to give Notion another try in the future and will gladly change the rating to 4 or 5 stars if the issue is fixed. I love the concept behind it, but with its current state I would rather use Apple Notes.

Requesting a feature Notion is an incredible tool, but it's really hard to use on ipad specially 12.9 inch, because the default font size and icons are too small. How is any app meant to grow if people give it 1-star basically because it’s new?

Personally, I also prefer that data is kept in the cloud, and not on my device, because that keeps the apps lightweight (and in theory, consistent across platforms, but this is not the case) and I’m sure greatly improves the ease of maintenance, which means developers can focus on improving features instead.

That said, Notion is not keeping the iPad app up to date with all of the desktop features, such as Header 1/2/3/ toggle lists are not supported in the iPad app, I cannot expand the toggle, it just shows as a heading instead. I find this a little surprising, considering this is basically just a web app, but it’s not a big deal. It does show that their development process is not completely watertight though.

This just means, I will continue to use Notion in Safari on the iPad, it’s not a big deal, and I would rather Notion keeps innovating and adds iPad features when they have resources too.

强制推送AI功能，破坏用户体验 之前的 Notion 页面简洁美观，功能实用，能让用户专注于笔记本身。然而，更新后的版本在每个页面底部强制添加了三个丑陋且干扰视线的按钮：
1. 搜索按钮（重复功能，原本已有快捷键和顶部搜索框）；
2. 占据最大空间的AI按钮（明显为推销其不实用的AI功能，并不好用）；
3. 新建页面按钮（同样重复）。

问题核心：
•强制推销：AI 功能非但不好用，还占用界面空间，严重影响笔记的视觉整洁性。
•无法关闭：未提供用户自定义隐藏这些按钮的选项，用户选择权。
